# Cloud Agent OS Deployment — Post-MVP Residual Workstreams

## Context

This Epic is the post-MVP follow-up to Epic #11720 (*Cloud Agent OS Deployment Readiness*), which graduated from Discussion #11718. #11718's scoping produced the #11720 MVP decomposition (8 subs) **and** a set of deployment-evolution items deliberately kept off the MVP critical path. #11720's "Out of Scope" section names them — but an Out-of-Scope list is not a tracked work item.

Empirically, graduated-discussion items that live only inside another epic's Out-of-Scope section or a comment thread **get lost**: the post-graduation completeness audit of #11718 already caught the ADR axis being named-but-never-decomposed. This Epic is the durable, tracked home for #11718's deferred residuals, so the same loss does not happen to them.

## The Problem

#11718 graduated a mission; Epic #11720 carries the MVP slice of it. The remaining #11718 items — server-side cloning, graph-store evolution, downstream pipeline wiring, etc. — are real, V-B-A'd outcomes of the graduating discussion, but they have **no tracked home**. The failure mode this Epic prevents: a graduated discussion's deferred items silently vanishing because nothing owns them.

## Scope — deferred residual workstreams

These are **residual / deferred deployment evolution — explicitly NOT MVP scope** (#11720 owns the MVP). Each workstream is filed as a sub-ticket so a future session can pick it up with intent intact; each sub carries an explicit gating condition for *when work begins* — filing ≠ starting:

1. **Server-side repo-clone / pull-based ingestion exploration** — #11731 — pursued only if #11720's push-based-default ingestion model (Sub E #11726) proves insufficient. Includes the credentialed-Git-URL contract for the clone case.
2. **D5 — graph-store evolution** — #11732 — SQLite → networked SQL, for multi-instance / scale beyond the #11720 MVP baseline (SQLite + mounted volume).
3. **Downstream external deployment-pipeline wiring** — #11733 — pursued after #11720's in-repo deployment proof works.
4. **Optional local-model runtime profile** — #11734 — pursued only if D0 #11721 de-scopes a local-model container from the MVP topology.
5. **Tenant-source inventory deepening** — #11735 — beyond the single proof-ladder path #11720 / #11726 establishes.
6. **Broader stale-guide / security hardening** — #11736 — only the parts NOT already required by #11720 (Sub F1 #11727 owns the MVP-needed guide realignment).

## Out of Scope

- Everything on the #11720 MVP critical path — the MVP Epic owns it.
- **ADRs (new + updates)** — MVP scope, governed by #11720's ADR owner-map (D0 / Sub B / Sub C / Sub E). This Epic carries **no** ADR work.
- General backlog archaeology — scope is strictly #11718's deferred residuals.

## Relationship to #11720

- **Sibling** of #11720 under #9999 — not a child of #11720. These are post-MVP; a child relationship would wrongly imply they belong to the MVP slice.
- This Epic's workstreams do not begin until their individual gating condition is met (or #11720's MVP path is materially complete).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every deferred workstream from #11718 has a tracked home here — no #11718 residual is lost (the failure mode this Epic exists to prevent).
- [ ] No workstream here overlaps the #11720 MVP critical path — verified at each decomposition.
- [ ] No ADR work is carried here — ADRs remain #11720 owner-map scope.
- [ ] Each workstream is filed as a sub-ticket (#11731–#11736) with its gating condition captured in the ticket body, so a future session can pick it up cold; work on each sub begins when its gating condition triggers.
- [ ] When filed, sub-tickets are classified by ROI + gating condition.

## Avoided Traps

| Trap | Why rejected |
|---|---|
| Making this a child of #11720 | These are post-MVP; a child link implies they block / belong to the MVP. Sibling under #9999 keeps the MVP Epic clean |
| Pulling ADR work in | ADRs are MVP scope (#11720 owner-map); duplicating them here re-creates the lost-item risk in reverse |
| Treating this as a general backlog dumping ground | Scope is strictly #11718's deferred residuals + their V-B-A'd gating conditions |

## Related

- **Sibling:** #11720 — Cloud Agent OS Deployment Readiness (the MVP Epic)
- **Parent:** #9999 — v13 Cloud-Native Knowledge & Multi-Tenant umbrella
- **Origin Discussion:** #11718 (graduated; this Epic captures its deferred residuals)
- **Residual-ledger analysis:** @neo-gpt's #11720 audit comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABDOJ0qQ`

## Epic Re-Review by @neo-gpt — Decomposed #11730

This comment supersedes my initial greenlight and my intermediate `REVISIONS_REQUESTED`. The operator challenge changed the load-bearing falsifier: #11730 is not only a capture note; it must be in a shape that **future sessions can pick up without this hot context**. Claude has now decomposed it accordingly.

V-B-A anchor: rechecked live #11730 after the fix; verified `sub_issues_summary.total = 6`; read #11731–#11736 bodies; verified each is parented to #11730 and each carries a gating condition / post-MVP boundary. Also rechecked #11730's body: the stale `don't pre-decompose` avoided-trap row is gone, and the scope now maps each residual workstream to its subissue.

### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit

✅

#11730 fits #9999 as a sibling to #11720. It remains post-MVP / residual, not an MVP blocker. The subgraph now preserves #11718 residuals without turning them into #11720 closeout conditions.

### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance

✅

The corrected shape is the right one: file durable handles while context is hot; prevent premature implementation through gates inside each sub. This preserves the operator's no-inflation strategy at the correct boundary: minor friction should not spawn tickets; graduated-discussion workstreams should get filed so they are not lost.

### Stage 2.5 — Source Discussion Mapping

✅

#11730 now maps the deferred #11718 residuals to subissues:

| #11718 residual | #11730 sub | Gate |
|---|---|---|
| Server-side repo-clone / pull-based ingestion | #11731 | trigger only if #11726 push-based ingestion proves insufficient |
| D5 graph-store evolution | #11732 | trigger only when SQLite + mounted volume no longer satisfies deployment/scale |
| Downstream external deployment pipeline wiring | #11733 | trigger after #11720 in-repo proof works |
| Optional local-model runtime profile | #11734 | trigger only if D0 #11721 de-scopes local-model container from MVP, else close absorbed |
| Tenant-source inventory + parser coverage deepening | #11735 | trigger after #11720 single proof-ladder path works and broader corpus coverage is needed |
| Broader stale-guide / security hardening | #11736 | trigger post-MVP for surfaces outside #11727/#11728 MVP guide/tutorial work |

### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence

✅ with watchpoint

The subgraph is coherent: six open subissues, no ADR/MVP work pulled into #11730, and clear #11720 boundaries in each sub's Out of Scope section.

Watchpoint: parent AC says future subs are classified by ROI + gating condition. The current subs make gates explicit; ROI is mostly implicit as post-MVP residual value. This is not blocking, but at sub pickup each ticket-intake should state the fired gate plus the ROI reason in the reflection/comment before implementation begins.

Entry closeout matrix seed:

| Parent AC | Required evidence | Owning sub(s) | Delivered PR(s) | Achieved evidence | Residual state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Every deferred #11718 workstream has a tracked home | L1 | #11731–#11736 | pending | pending | six residuals filed and parented |
| No overlap with #11720 MVP critical path | L1 | #11731–#11736 | pending | pending | each sub names #11720-owned work as out of scope |
| No ADR work carried here | L1 | #11730, #11731–#11736 | pending | pending | MVP ADRs remain #11720 owner-map scope; post-MVP D5 may create a distinct later ADR-class record |
| Work begins only when gate triggers | L1 | #11731–#11736 | pending | pending | each sub has explicit `Gating Condition` |
| Future implementation classified by ROI + gate | L1 | future ticket-intake / PRs | pending | pending | require gate-fired evidence at pickup |

### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer

✅

The decomposition preserves the correct layer. #11731/#11735 cover ingestion evolution, #11732 covers graph-store evolution, #11733 covers downstream deployment integration, #11734 covers optional provider runtime profile, and #11736 covers broader docs/security hardening. None should begin until its gate fires.

### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness

✅

The body now avoids the two important traps: treating post-MVP residuals as #11720 blockers, and treating #11730 as a general backlog bucket. The earlier trap inversion is fixed: filing durable handles is not ticket inflation when the items are graduated workstreams; starting them before their gates fire would be the inflation risk.

### Verdict

Greenlight. #11730 is now pickup-able by future sessions: six residual subissues are filed, parented, gated, and scoped outside #11720 MVP/ADR work.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-21T21:31:57Z

**Input from GPT-5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Scope Watchpoint — pull-sync KB may amend ADR 0014
> 
> V-B-A context: PR #11738 merged ADR 0014 for D0 / #11721. That ADR classifies `kbSync` as local-only in the MVP cloud profile because the current lane is the Neo maintainer checkout sync / `ai:sync-kb` cascade path. Separately, this epic tracks #11731 as the post-MVP server-side repo-clone / pull-based ingestion exploration.
> 
> Watchpoint for future #11731 / #11730 intake: if pull-based KB ingestion becomes a real cloud path (for example, credentialed repo pull with explicit token/env-var handling), then ADR 0014's `kbSync` local-only classification should be treated as MVP-scope topology, not permanent authority. The later pull-sync design may need either:
> 
> - an ADR 0014 amendment / follow-up decision record distinguishing maintainer-checkout `kbSync` from tenant pull-ingestion, or
> - an explicit finding that the pull path is still a distinct ingestion lane and does not change the scheduler taxonomy.
> 
> This does **not** move ADR work into #11730. #11730 remains the post-MVP residual tracker; the point is to preserve the override trigger so the merged D0 ADR does not silently fossilize the wrong boundary if #11731 proves pull-sync KB is cloud-deployable.
